text: The primary outcome, a composite of cardiovascular death or hospitalization for heart failure, occurred in 361 of 1863 patients (19.4%) in the empagliflozin group and in 462 of 1867 patients (24.7%) in the placebo group (hazard ratio, 0.75; 95% confidence interval [CI], 0.65 to 0.86; P<0.001).

A primary outcome event occurred in 415 of 2997 patients (13.8%) in the empagliflozin group and in 511 of 2991 patients (17.1%) in the placebo group (hazard ratio, 0.79; 95% CI, 0.69 to 0.90; P<0.001).
The primary outcome occurred in 386 of 2373 patients (16.3%) in the dapagliflozin group and in 502 of 2371 patients (21.2%) in the placebo group (hazard ratio, 0.74; 95% CI, 0.65 to 0.85; P<0.001).
The primary outcome occurred in 914 patients (21.8%) in the LCZ696 group and 1117 patients (26.5%) in the enalapril group (hazard ratio, 0.80; 95% confidence interval [CI], 0.73 to 0.87; P<0.001).

The primary outcome occurred in 490 of 4687 patients (10.5%) in the pooled empagliflozin group and in 282 of 2333 patients (12.1%) in the placebo group (hazard ratio in the empagliflozin group, 0.86; 95% CI, 0.74 to 0.99; P=0.04).
Dapagliflozin did not result in a lower rate of MACE (8.8% in the dapagliflozin group and 9.4% in the placebo group; hazard ratio, 0.93; 95% CI, 0.84 to 1.03; P=0.17).
The rate of the primary outcome was lower with canagliflozin than with placebo (occurring in 26.9 vs. 31.5 participants per 1000 patient-years; hazard ratio, 0.86; 95% CI, 0.75 to 0.97; P=0.02).
The relative risk of the primary outcome was 30% lower in the canagliflozin group than in the placebo group (hazard ratio, 0.70; 95% confidence interval [CI], 0.59 to 0.82; P=0.00001).
The primary outcome occurred in 504 of 2833 patients (17.8%) in the finerenone group and in 600 of 2841 patients (21.1%) in the placebo group (hazard ratio, 0.82; 95% CI, 0.73 to 0.93; P=0.001).
A primary outcome event occurred in 458 of 3686 patients (12.4%) in the finerenone group and in 519 of 3666 patients (14.2%) in the placebo group (hazard ratio, 0.87; 95% CI, 0.76 to 0.98; P=0.03).
The primary outcome occurred in 897 of 2526 patients (35.5%) in the vericiguat group and in 972 of 2524 patients (38.5%) in the placebo group (hazard ratio, 0.90; 95% CI, 0.82 to 0.98; P=0.02).

The primary outcome occurred in 1096 of 4120 patients (26.6%) in the omecamtiv mecarbil group and in 1222 of 4112 patients (29.7%) in the placebo group (hazard ratio, 0.92; 95% confidence interval [CI], 0.86 to 0.99; P=0.03).
The primary outcome occurred in 512 of 3131 patients (16.4%) in the dapagliflozin group and in 610 of 3132 patients (19.5%) in the placebo group (hazard ratio, 0.82; 95% CI, 0.73 to 0.92; P<0.001).
A primary end-point event occurred in 217 patients (9.6%) with sotagliflozin and in 269 patients (12.0%) with placebo (hazard ratio, 0.74; 95% CI, 0.63 to 0.88; P<0.001).
The primary end point occurred in 51 patients (11.0%) in the sotagliflozin group and in 76 patients (16.3%) in the placebo group (hazard ratio, 0.67; 95% CI, 0.52 to 0.85; P<0.001).
All-cause mortality was 29.5% with tafamidis and 42.9% with placebo (hazard ratio, 0.70; 95% CI, 0.51 to 0.96).

The primary outcome occurred in 320 of 1722 patients (18.6%) in the spironolactone group and 351 of 1723 patients (20.4%) in the placebo group (hazard ratio, 0.89; 95% confidence interval [CI], 0.77 to 1.04; P=0.14).
The primary endpoint occurred in 793 (24%) patients in the ivabradine group versus 937 (29%) in the placebo group (hazard ratio 0.82, 95% CI 0.75-0.90, p<0.0001).
The primary outcome occurred in 249 patients (18.3%) in the eplerenone group versus 356 patients (25.9%) in the placebo group (hazard ratio, 0.63; 95% CI, 0.54 to 0.74; P<0.001).
The primary outcome occurred in 770 (32.9%) patients in the combination group compared with 808 (34.6%) patients in the enalapril group (hazard ratio 0.93, 95% CI 0.85-1.03, p=0.17).
